# Markdown Instructions

## Purpose
Ensure all markdown files are clean, structured, and production-ready.

---

## Structure Rules
- Use proper heading hierarchy (# → ## → ###)
- No skipping heading levels
- Group related content into sections
- Keep sections concise and readable

---

## Spacing Rules
- No excessive blank lines
- No double spacing between short sections
- Keep layout tight and clean

---

## Lists
- Use consistent bullet formatting
- Do not mix styles (-, *, etc.)
- Keep indentation clean

---

## Code Blocks
- Always use fenced code blocks
- Include language when possible
- Close all code blocks properly
- Do not leave partial snippets

---

## Cleanup Rules
- Remove duplicate sections
- Remove placeholder text
- Fix incomplete sections
- Convert rough notes into structured content

---

## Enhancement Rules
When appropriate:
- Convert text into tables
- Add section headers
- Improve readability
- Organize content logically

---

## Do NOT
- Add filler content
- Overwrite meaningful content
- Introduce unnecessary sections
- Leave markdown partially fixed

---

## Goal
All markdown should:
- Read clean
- Be structured
- Be easy to scan
- Be immediately usable
